key_insight: Scaling up GPT-style language models delays plasticity loss, but does not eliminate it, and even stationary training can eventually reduce a model’s ability to learn new information.
engineering_relevance: For continual-learning and long-horizon deployment of LLMs, bigger models alone are not a reliable fix; maintaining adaptability likely requires training or architectural interventions beyond parameter scaling.
method: Studied GPT-style Transformers on a multilingual continual-learning setup.
Compared models from 5M to 314M non-embedding parameters.
Tracked plasticity using performance deterioration on a held-out Vietnamese probing task, including stationary multilingual training as a control.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a clear deterioration on a held-out Vietnamese probe across model sizes, with the onset of plasticity loss following a sublinear scaling law.
watch_item: The results suggest that scale only delays plasticity loss, so practitioners should not assume larger LLMs will remain equally adaptable over long training or deployment.
paper_title: Can Scale Save Us From Plasticity Loss in Large Language Models?
